NKJV.1: Hear, my children, the instruction of a father, And give attention to know understanding;
NKJV.2: For I give you good doctrine: Do not forsake my law.
NKJV.3: When I was my father’s son,  Tender and the only one in the sight of my mother,
NKJV.4: He also taught me, and said to me: “Let your heart retain my words;  Keep my commands, and live.
NKJV.5: Get wisdom! Get understanding! Do not forget, nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
NKJV.6: Do not forsake her, and she will preserve you;  Love her, and she will keep you.
NKJV.7: Wisdom is the principal thing; Therefore get wisdom. And in all your getting, get understanding.
NKJV.8: Exalt her, and she will promote you; She will bring you honor, when you embrace her.
NKJV.9: She will place on your head an ornament of grace; A crown of glory she will deliver to you.”
NKJV.10: Hear, my son, and receive my sayings,  And the years of your life will be many.
NKJV.11: I have taught you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in right paths.
NKJV.12: When you walk, your steps will not be hindered,  And when you run, you will not stumble.
NKJV.13: Take firm hold of instruction, do not let go; Keep her, for she is your life.
NKJV.14: Do not enter the path of the wicked, And do not walk in the way of evil.
NKJV.15: Avoid it, do not travel on it; Turn away from it and pass on.
NKJV.16: For they do not sleep unless they have done evil; And their sleep is taken away unless they make someone fall.
NKJV.17: For they eat the bread of wickedness, And drink the wine of violence.
NKJV.18: But the path of the just is like the shining sun, That shines ever brighter unto the perfect day.
NKJV.19: The way of the wicked is like darkness; They do not know what makes them stumble.
NKJV.20: My son, give attention to my words; Incline your ear to my sayings.
NKJV.21: Do not let them depart from your eyes; Keep them in the midst of your heart;
NKJV.22: For they are life to those who find them, And health to all their flesh.
NKJV.23: Keep your heart with all diligence, For out of it spring the issues of life.
NKJV.24: Put away from you a deceitful mouth, And put perverse lips far from you.
NKJV.25: Let your eyes look straight ahead, And your eyelids look right before you.
NKJV.26: Ponder the path of your feet, And let all your ways be established.
NKJV.27: Do not turn to the right or the left; Remove your foot from evil.

ESV.1: Hear, O sons, a father's instruction, and be attentive, that you may gain  insight,
ESV.2: for I give you good precepts; do not forsake my teaching.
ESV.3: When I was a son with my father, tender, the only one in the sight of my mother,
ESV.4: he taught me and said to me, “Let your heart hold fast my words; keep my commandments, and live.
ESV.5: Get wisdom; get insight; do not forget, and do not turn away from the words of my mouth.
ESV.6: Do not forsake her, and she will keep you; love her, and she will guard you.
ESV.7: The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom, and whatever you get, get insight.
ESV.8: Prize her highly, and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
ESV.9: She will place on your head a graceful garland; she will bestow on you a beautiful crown.”
ESV.10: Hear, my son, and accept my words, that the years of your life may be many.
ESV.11: I have taught you the way of wisdom; I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
ESV.12: When you walk, your step will not be hampered, and if you run, you will not stumble.
ESV.13: Keep hold of instruction; do not let go; guard her, for she is your life.
ESV.14: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of the evil.
ESV.15: Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
ESV.16: For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
ESV.17: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
ESV.18: But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
ESV.19: The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.
ESV.20: My son, be attentive to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.
ESV.21: Let them not escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
ESV.22: For they are life to those who find them, and healing to all their  flesh.
ESV.23: Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.
ESV.24: Put away from you crooked speech, and put devious talk far from you.
ESV.25: Let your eyes look directly forward, and your gaze be straight before you.
ESV.26: Ponder  the path of your feet; then all your ways will be sure.
ESV.27: Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.

NASB.1: Hear, [O] sons, the instruction of a father, And give attention that you may gain understanding,
NASB.2: For I give you sound teaching; Do not abandon my instruction.
NASB.3: When I was a son to my father, Tender and the only son in the sight of my mother,
NASB.4: Then he taught me and said to me, 'Let your heart hold fast my words; Keep my commandments and live;
NASB.5: Acquire wisdom! Acquire understanding! Do not forget nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
NASB.6: 'Do not forsake her, and she will guard you; Love her, and she will watch over you.
NASB.7: 'The beginning of wisdom [is]: Acquire wisdom; And with all your acquiring, get understanding.
NASB.8: 'Prize her, and she will exalt you; She will honor you if you embrace her.
NASB.9: 'She will place on your head a garland of grace; She will present you with a crown of beauty.'
NASB.10: Hear, my son, and accept my sayings And the years of your life will be many.
NASB.11: I have directed you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in upright paths.
NASB.12: When you walk, your steps will not be impeded; And if you run, you will not stumble.
NASB.13: Take hold of instruction; do not let go. Guard her, for she is your life.
NASB.14: Do not enter the path of the wicked And do not proceed in the way of evil men.
NASB.15: Avoid it, do not pass by it; Turn away from it and pass on.
NASB.16: For they cannot sleep unless they do evil; And they are robbed of sleep unless they make [someone] stumble.
NASB.17: For they eat the bread of wickedness And drink the wine of violence.
NASB.18: But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, That shines brighter and brighter until the full day.
NASB.19: The way of the wicked is like darkness; They do not know over what they stumble.
NASB.20: My son, give attention to my words; Incline your ear to my sayings.
NASB.21: Do not let them depart from your sight; Keep them in the midst of your heart.
NASB.22: For they are life to those who find them And health to all their body.
NASB.23: Watch over your heart with all diligence, For from it [flow] the springs of life.
NASB.24: Put away from you a deceitful mouth And put devious speech far from you.
NASB.25: Let your eyes look directly ahead And let your gaze be fixed straight in front of you.
NASB.26: Watch the path of your feet And all your ways will be established.
NASB.27: Do not turn to the right nor to the left; Turn your foot from evil.

NLT.1:
My children, listen when your father corrects you.
Pay attention and learn good judgment,
NLT.2:
for I am giving you good guidance.
Don’t turn away from my instructions.
NLT.3:
For I, too, was once my father’s son,
tenderly loved as my mother’s only child.
NLT.4:
My father taught me,
“Take my words to heart.
Follow my commands, and you will live.
NLT.5:
Get wisdom; develop good judgment.
Don’t forget my words or turn away from them.
NLT.6:
Don’t turn your back on wisdom, for she will protect you.
Love her, and she will guard you.
NLT.7:
Getting wisdom is the wisest thing you can do!
And whatever else you do, develop good judgment.
NLT.8:
If you prize wisdom, she will make you great.
Embrace her, and she will honor you.
NLT.9:
She will place a lovely wreath on your head;
she will present you with a beautiful crown.”
NLT.10:
My child, listen to me and do as I say,
and you will have a long, good life.
NLT.11:
I will teach you wisdom’s ways
and lead you in straight paths.
NLT.12:
When you walk, you won’t be held back;
when you run, you won’t stumble.
NLT.13:
Take hold of my instructions; don’t let them go.
Guard them, for they are the key to life.
NLT.14:
Don’t do as the wicked do,
and don’t follow the path of evildoers.
NLT.15:
Don’t even think about it; don’t go that way.
Turn away and keep moving.
NLT.16:
For evil people can’t sleep until they’ve done their evil deed for the day.
They can’t rest until they’ve caused someone to stumble.
NLT.17:
They eat the food of wickedness
and drink the wine of violence!
NLT.18:
The way of the righteous is like the first gleam of dawn,
which shines ever brighter until the full light of day.
NLT.19:
But the way of the wicked is like total darkness.
They have no idea what they are stumbling over.
NLT.20:
My child, pay attention to what I say.
Listen carefully to my words.
NLT.21:
Don’t lose sight of them.
Let them penetrate deep into your heart,
NLT.22:
for they bring life to those who find them,
and healing to their whole body.
NLT.23:
Guard your heart above all else,
for it determines the course of your life.
NLT.24:
Avoid all perverse talk;
stay away from corrupt speech.
NLT.25:
Look straight ahead,
and fix your eyes on what lies before you.
NLT.26:
Mark out a straight path for your feet;
stay on the safe path.
NLT.27:
Don’t get sidetracked;
keep your feet from following evil.

WEB.1: Listen, sons, to a father’s instruction. Pay attention and know understanding;
WEB.2: for I give you sound learning. Don’t forsake my law.
WEB.3: For I was a son to my father, tender and an only child in the sight of my mother.
WEB.4: He taught me, and said to me: “Let your heart retain my words. Keep my commandments, and live.
WEB.5: Get wisdom. Get understanding. Don’t forget, neither swerve from the words of my mouth.
WEB.6: Don’t forsake her, and she will preserve you. Love her, and she will keep you.
WEB.7: Wisdom is supreme. Get wisdom. Yes, though it costs all your possessions, get understanding.
WEB.8: Esteem her, and she will exalt you. She will bring you to honor, when you embrace her.
WEB.9: She will give to your head a garland of grace. She will deliver a crown of splendor to you.”
WEB.10: Listen, my son, and receive my sayings. The years of your life will be many.
WEB.11: I have taught you in the way of wisdom. I have led you in straight paths.
WEB.12: When you go, your steps will not be hampered. When you run, you will not stumble.
WEB.13: Take firm hold of instruction. Don’t let her go. Keep her, for she is your life.
WEB.14: Don’t enter into the path of the wicked. Don’t walk in the way of evil men.
WEB.15: Avoid it, and don’t pass by it. Turn from it, and pass on.
WEB.16: For they don’t sleep, unless they do evil. Their sleep is taken away, unless they make someone fall.
WEB.17: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
WEB.18: But the path of the righteous is like the dawning light, that shines more and more until the perfect day.
WEB.19: The way of the wicked is like darkness. They don’t know what they stumble over.
WEB.20: My son, attend to my words. Turn your ear to my sayings.
WEB.21: Let them not depart from your eyes. Keep them in the center of your heart.
WEB.22: For they are life to those who find them, and health to their whole body.
WEB.23: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.
WEB.24: Put away from yourself a perverse mouth. Put corrupt lips far from you.
WEB.25: Let your eyes look straight ahead. Fix your gaze directly before you.
WEB.26: Make the path of your feet level. Let all of your ways be established.
WEB.27: Don’t turn to the right hand nor to the left. Remove your foot from evil.

CSB17.1:
Listen, sons, to a father’s discipline, ⓚ
and pay attention so that you may gain understanding,
CSB17.2:
for I am giving you good instruction. ⓛ
Don’t abandon my teaching.
CSB17.3:
When I was a son with my father,
tender and precious to my mother,
CSB17.4:
he taught me and said:
“Your heart must hold on to my words.
Keep my commands and live. ⓜ
CSB17.5:
Get wisdom, get understanding; ⓝ
don’t forget or turn away from the words from my mouth.
CSB17.6:
Don’t abandon wisdom, and she will watch over you;
love her, ⓐ and she will guard you.
CSB17.7:
Wisdom is supreme — so get wisdom.
And whatever else you get, get understanding. ⓑ
CSB17.8:
Cherish her, and she will exalt you;
if you embrace her, she will honor you. ⓒ
CSB17.9:
She will place a garland of favor on your head;
she will give you a crown of beauty.” ⓓ
CSB17.10:
Listen, my son. Accept my words, ⓔ
and you will live many years. ⓕ
CSB17.11:
I am teaching you the way of wisdom;
I am guiding you on straight paths. ⓖ
CSB17.12:
When you walk, your steps will not be hindered; ⓗ
when you run, you will not stumble. ⓘ
CSB17.13:
Hold on to instruction; don’t let go. ⓙ
Guard it, for it is your life.
CSB17.14:
Keep off the path of the wicked; ⓚ
don’t proceed on the way of evil ones.
CSB17.15:
Avoid it; don’t travel on it.
Turn away from it, and pass it by.
CSB17.16:
For they can’t sleep
unless they have done what is evil;
they are robbed of sleep
unless they make someone stumble. ⓛ
CSB17.17:
They eat the bread of wickedness
and drink the wine of violence.
CSB17.18:
The path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, ⓜ
shining brighter and brighter until midday.
CSB17.19:
But the way of the wicked is like the darkest gloom; ⓝ
they don’t know what makes them stumble. ⓐ
CSB17.20:
My son, pay attention to my words;
listen closely to my sayings. ⓑ
CSB17.21:
Don’t lose sight of them; ⓒ
keep them within your heart.
CSB17.22:
For they are life to those who find them,
and health to one’s whole body.
CSB17.23:
Guard your heart above all else, [1]
for it is the source of life. ⓓ
CSB17.24:
Don’t let your mouth speak dishonestly, ⓔ
and don’t let your lips talk deviously.
CSB17.25:
Let your eyes look forward;
fix your gaze [2] straight ahead. ⓕ
CSB17.26:
Carefully consider the path [3] for your feet, ⓖ
and all your ways will be established.
CSB17.27:
Don’t turn to the right or to the left; ⓗ
keep your feet away from evil.

AMP.1:
Hear, O children, the instruction of a father,
And pay attention [and be willing to learn] so that you may gain understanding and intelligent discernment.
AMP.2:
For I give you good doctrine;
Do not turn away from my instruction.
AMP.3:
When I was a son with my father (David),
Tender and the only son in the sight of my mother (Bathsheba),
AMP.4:
He taught me and said to me,
"Let your heart hold fast my words;
Keep my commandments and live.
AMP.5:
"Get [skillful and godly] wisdom! Acquire understanding [actively seek spiritual discernment, mature comprehension, and logical interpretation]!
Do not forget nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
AMP.6:
"Do not turn away from her (Wisdom) and she will guard and protect you;
Love her, and she will watch over you.
AMP.7:
"The beginning of wisdom is: Get [skillful and godly] wisdom [it is preeminent]!
And with all your acquiring, get understanding [actively seek spiritual discernment, mature comprehension, and logical interpretation].
AMP.8:
"Prize wisdom [and exalt her], and she will exalt you;
She will honor you if you embrace her.
AMP.9:
"She will place on your head a garland of grace;
She will present you with a crown of beauty and glory."
AMP.10:
Hear, my son, and accept my sayings,
And the years of your life will be many.
AMP.11:
I have instructed you in the way of [skillful and godly] wisdom;
I have led you in upright paths.
AMP.12:
When you walk, your steps will not be impeded [for your path will be clear and open];
And when you run, you will not stumble.
AMP.13:
Take hold of instruction; [actively seek it, grip it firmly and] do not let go.
Guard her, for she is your life.
AMP.14:
Do not enter the path of the wicked,
And do not go the way of evil men.
AMP.15:
Avoid it, do not travel on it;
Turn away from it and pass on.
AMP.16:
For the wicked cannot sleep unless they do evil;
And they are deprived of sleep unless they make someone stumble and fall.
AMP.17:
For they eat the bread of wickedness
And drink the wine of violence.
AMP.18:
But the path of the just (righteous) is like the light of dawn,
That shines brighter and brighter until [it reaches its full strength and glory in] the perfect day.
AMP.19:
The way of the wicked is like [deep] darkness;
They do not know over what they stumble.
AMP.20:
My son, pay attention to my words and be willing to learn;
Open your ears to my sayings.
AMP.21:
Do not let them escape from your sight;
Keep them in the center of your heart.
AMP.22:
For they are life to those who find them,
And healing and health to all their flesh.
AMP.23:
Watch over your heart with all diligence,
For from it flow the springs of life.
AMP.24:
Put away from you a deceitful (lying, misleading) mouth,
And put devious lips far from you.
AMP.25:
Let your eyes look directly ahead [toward the path of moral courage]
And let your gaze be fixed straight in front of you [toward the path of integrity].
AMP.26:
Consider well and watch carefully the path of your feet,
And all your ways will be steadfast and sure.
AMP.27:
Do not turn away to the right nor to the left [where evil may lurk];
Turn your foot from [the path of] evil.

MSG.1:
Listen, friends, to some fatherly advice;
sit up and take notice so you'll know how to live.
MSG.2:
I'm giving you good counsel;
don't let it go in one ear and out the other.
MSG.3:
When I was a boy at my father's knee,
the pride and joy of my mother,
MSG.4:
He would sit me down and drill me:
"Take this to heart. Do what I tell you—live!
MSG.5:
Sell everything and buy Wisdom! Forage for Understanding!
Don't forget one word! Don't deviate an inch!
MSG.6:
Never walk away from Wisdom—she guards your life;
love her—she keeps her eye on you.
MSG.7:
Above all and before all, do this: Get Wisdom!
Write this at the top of your list: Get Understanding!
MSG.8:
Throw your arms around her—believe me, you won't regret it;
never let her go—she'll make your life glorious.
MSG.9:
She'll garland your life with grace,
she'll festoon your days with beauty."
MSG.10:
Dear friend, take my advice;
it will add years to your life.
MSG.11:
I'm writing out clear directions to Wisdom Way,
I'm drawing a map to Righteous Road.
MSG.12:
I don't want you ending up in blind alleys,
or wasting time making wrong turns.
MSG.13:
Hold tight to good advice; don't relax your grip.
Guard it well—your life is at stake!
MSG.14:
Don't take Wicked Bypass;
don't so much as set foot on that road.
MSG.15:
Stay clear of it; give it a wide berth.
Make a detour and be on your way.
MSG.16:
Evil people are restless
unless they're making trouble;
They can't get a good night's sleep
unless they've made life miserable for somebody.
MSG.17:
Perversity is their food and drink,
violence their drug of choice.
MSG.18:
The ways of right-living people glow with light;
the longer they live, the brighter they shine.
MSG.19:
But the road of wrongdoing gets darker and darker—
travelers can't see a thing; they fall flat on their faces.
MSG.20:
Dear friend, listen well to my words;
tune your ears to my voice.
MSG.21:
Keep my message in plain view at all times.
Concentrate! Learn it by heart!
MSG.22:
Those who discover these words live, really live;
body and soul, they're bursting with health.
MSG.23:
Keep vigilant watch over your heart;
that's where life starts.
MSG.24:
Don't talk out of both sides of your mouth;
avoid careless banter, white lies, and gossip.
MSG.25:
Keep your eyes straight ahead;
ignore all sideshow distractions.
MSG.26:
Watch your step,
and the road will stretch out smooth before you.
MSG.27:
Look neither right nor left;
leave evil in the dust.

NIV.1:
Wisdom Is Supreme
Listen, my sons, to a father’s instruction;
pay attention and gain understanding.
NIV.2:
I give you sound learning,
so do not forsake my teaching.
NIV.3:
When I was a boy in my father’s house,
still tender, and an only child of my mother,
NIV.4:
he taught me and said,
“Lay hold of my words with all your heart;
keep my commands and you will live.
NIV.5:
Get wisdom, get understanding;
do not forget my words or swerve from them.
NIV.6:
Do not forsake wisdom, and she will protect you;
love her, and she will watch over you.
NIV.7:
Wisdom is supreme; therefore get wisdom.
Though it cost all you have, get understanding.
NIV.8:
Esteem her, and she will exalt you;
embrace her, and she will honor you.
NIV.9:
She will set a garland of grace on your head
and present you with a crown of splendor.”
NIV.10:
Listen, my son, accept what I say,
and the years of your life will be many.
NIV.11:
I guide you in the way of wisdom
and lead you along straight paths.
NIV.12:
When you walk, your steps will not be hampered;
when you run, you will not stumble.
NIV.13:
Hold on to instruction, do not let it go;
guard it well, for it is your life.
NIV.14:
Do not set foot on the path of the wicked
or walk in the way of evil men.
NIV.15:
Avoid it, do not travel on it;
turn from it and go on your way.
NIV.16:
For they cannot sleep till they do evil;
they are robbed of slumber till they make someone fall.
NIV.17:
They eat the bread of wickedness
and drink the wine of violence.
NIV.18:
The path of the righteous is like the first gleam of dawn,
shining ever brighter till the full light of day.
NIV.19:
But the way of the wicked is like deep darkness;
they do not know what makes them stumble.
NIV.20:
My son, pay attention to what I say;
listen closely to my words.
NIV.21:
Do not let them out of your sight,
keep them within your heart;
NIV.22:
for they are life to those who find them
and health to a man’s whole body.
NIV.23:
Above all else, guard your heart,
for it is the wellspring of life.
NIV.24:
Put away perversity from your mouth;
keep corrupt talk far from your lips.
NIV.25:
Let your eyes look straight ahead,
fix your gaze directly before you.
NIV.26:
Make level paths for your feet
and take only ways that are firm.
NIV.27:
Do not swerve to the right or the left;
keep your foot from evil.

NRSVCE.1:
Listen, children, to a father’s instruction,
and be attentive, that you may gain insight;
NRSVCE.2:
for I give you good precepts:
do not forsake my teaching.
NRSVCE.3:
When I was a son with my father,
tender, and my mother’s favorite,
NRSVCE.4:
he taught me, and said to me,
“Let your heart hold fast my words;
keep my commandments, and live.
NRSVCE.5:
Get wisdom; get insight: do not forget, nor turn away
from the words of my mouth.
NRSVCE.6:
Do not forsake her, and she will keep you;
love her, and she will guard you.
NRSVCE.7:
The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom,
and whatever else you get, get insight.
NRSVCE.8:
Prize her highly, and she will exalt you;
she will honor you if you embrace her.
NRSVCE.9:
She will place on your head a fair garland;
she will bestow on you a beautiful crown.”
NRSVCE.10:
Hear, my child, and accept my words,
that the years of your life may be many.
NRSVCE.11:
I have taught you the way of wisdom;
I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
NRSVCE.12:
When you walk, your step will not be hampered;
and if you run, you will not stumble.
NRSVCE.13:
Keep hold of instruction; do not let go;
guard her, for she is your life.
NRSVCE.14:
Do not enter the path of the wicked,
and do not walk in the way of evildoers.
NRSVCE.15:
Avoid it; do not go on it;
turn away from it and pass on.
NRSVCE.16:
For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong;
they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
NRSVCE.17:
For they eat the bread of wickedness
and drink the wine of violence.
NRSVCE.18:
But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn,
which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
NRSVCE.19:
The way of the wicked is like deep darkness;
they do not know what they stumble over.
NRSVCE.20:
My child, be attentive to my words;
incline your ear to my sayings.
NRSVCE.21:
Do not let them escape from your sight;
keep them within your heart.
NRSVCE.22:
For they are life to those who find them,
and healing to all their flesh.
NRSVCE.23:
Keep your heart with all vigilance,
for from it flow the springs of life.
NRSVCE.24:
Put away from you crooked speech,
and put devious talk far from you.
NRSVCE.25:
Let your eyes look directly forward,
and your gaze be straight before you.
NRSVCE.26:
Keep straight the path of your feet,
and all your ways will be sure.
NRSVCE.27:
Do not swerve to the right or to the left;
turn your foot away from evil.

NET.1: Listen, children, to a father’s instruction, and pay attention so that you may gain discernment.
NET.2: Because I give you good instruction, do not forsake my teaching.
NET.3: When I was a son to my father, a tender only child before my mother,
NET.4: he taught me, and he said to me: “Let your heart lay hold of my words; keep my commands so that you will live.
NET.5: Acquire wisdom, acquire understanding; do not forget and do not turn aside from the words I speak.
NET.6: Do not forsake wisdom, and she will protect you; love her, and she will guard you.
NET.7: Wisdom is supreme — so acquire wisdom, and whatever you acquire, acquire understanding!
NET.8: Esteem her highly and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
NET.9: She will place a fair garland on your head; she will bestow a beautiful crown on you.”
NET.10: Listen, my child, and accept my words, so that the years of your life will be many.
NET.11: I will guide you in the way of wisdom and I will lead you in upright paths.
NET.12: When you walk, your steps will not be hampered, and when you run, you will not stumble.
NET.13: Hold on to instruction, do not let it go; protect it, because it is your life.
NET.14: Do not enter the path of the wicked or walk in the way of those who are evil.
NET.15: Avoid it, do not go on it; turn away from it, and go on.
NET.16: For they cannot sleep unless they cause harm; they are robbed of sleep until they make someone stumble.
NET.17: For they eat bread gained from wickedness and drink wine obtained from violence.
NET.18: But the path of the righteous is like the bright morning light, growing brighter and brighter until full day.
NET.19: The way of the wicked is like gloomy darkness; they do not know what causes them to stumble.
NET.20: My child, pay attention to my words; listen attentively to my sayings.
NET.21: Do not let them depart from your sight, guard them within your heart;
NET.22: for they are life to those who find them and healing to one’s entire body.
NET.23: Guard your heart with all vigilance, for from it are the sources of life.
NET.24: Remove perverse speech from your mouth; keep devious talk far from your lips.
NET.25: Let your eyes look directly in front of you and let your gaze look straight before you.
NET.26: Make the path for your feet level, so that all your ways may be established.
NET.27: Do not turn to the right or to the left; turn yourself away from evil.
